rejuvenationDownload.title: Pokémon Rejuvenation Download and Installation
rejuvenationDownload.intro: Pokémon Rejuvenation V14 is a free standalone fan game that does not require a Pokémon ROM or emulator. Download the complete package for your platform, extract every file before launching, and back up all V13.5 saves before attempting an upgrade.

cards.name: Windows Installation
cards.description: Download the V14 Windows package, extract the entire archive into a new folder, and launch the included game executable. Avoid running the game directly from the ZIP file or installing it inside a protected Windows system folder.

cards.name: macOS Installation
cards.description: Extract the macOS build and move the application to a writable folder such as Applications. If Gatekeeper blocks the first launch, use the Open command from the context menu instead of repeatedly double-clicking it.

cards.name: Linux Installation
cards.description: Extract the Linux package without changing its folder structure, give the included launcher executable permission, and start it from a writable directory. Missing-library errors usually mean the archive was only partially extracted.

cards.name: Kirin and JoiPlay
cards.description: Use the V14 Kirin package through the Kirin launcher, or install JoiPlay with its RPG Maker plugin and add the extracted Windows game. Mobile performance varies by Android device and is not identical to the native desktop builds.

cards.name: V13.5 Save Backup and Migration
cards.description: Back up the complete save folder before opening V14. Copy the original files rather than moving them, place a copy where V14 expects save data, and keep the untouched backup until the converted save loads correctly.

cards.name: Using the V14 Updater
cards.description: Run the included updater while the game is closed to apply small fixes without reinstalling the full package. Do not interrupt the updater or mix files from different V14 builds manually.

troubleshootingTips: Install the fonts included with the game when text is missing or characters appear blank.
Re-extract a clean archive when files cannot be found or audio errors appear.
Check antivirus quarantine for deleted executables or DLL files.
Close overlays and background recording tools if the screen freezes or performance drops.
Never combine V13.5 game files with the V14 installation.

rejuvenationWalkthrough.title: Pokémon Rejuvenation V14 Walkthrough
rejuvenationWalkthrough.intro: The V14 main story moves between eastern Aevium, Terajuma Island, Grand Dream City, ruined regions, and locations tied to Aevium's past. Because later scenes react to Karma, sidequests, relationships, and major choices, keep multiple save files and complete local content before leaving each major region.

steps.title: East Gearen City and Goldenwood
steps.description: Complete the opening journey, reach East Gearen City, choose a Gen 1-9 starter at the laboratory, and learn the basic systems before investigating the first Team Xen activity around Goldenwood Forest.
steps.locations: S.S. Oceana, Gearen Laboratory, Goldenwood Forest
steps.battles: Opening rival battles and first Team Xen encounters

steps.title: Route 2 and Sheridan Village
steps.description: Travel through Route 2 and Amethyst Cave to reach Sheridan Village, investigate the village crisis, and prepare for battles that emphasize Fighting-type pressure and changing Field Effects.
steps.battles: Keta-related battles and the Sheridan gym objective

steps.title: Goldenleaf Mansion Progression
steps.description: Continue west through story locations connected to the mansion and Goldenleaf region, resolve the supernatural events blocking progress, and prepare teams that work under Ghost-focused indoor Field Effects.
steps.locations: Goldenleaf Town, Goldenleaf Forest, Goldenleaf Theatre
steps.battles: Story boss battles and a major gym battle

steps.title: Terajuma Island and Kakori Village
steps.description: Explore Terajuma Island, establish access to Kakori Village, and work with local allies against Team Xen across large outdoor areas with new wild Pokémon, sidequests, and Crests.
steps.locations: Terajuma Island, Kakori Village, Terajuma Jungle
steps.battles: Kakori story battles and island gym objectives

steps.title: Valor Mountain Operations
steps.description: Advance through the volcanic region and disrupt Team Xen plans around Valor Mountain. Bring answers for Fire, Rock, and terrain-enhanced attacks across several consecutive battles.
steps.locations: Valor Mountain, volcanic caves, Teila Resort
steps.battles: Team Xen commanders and a Rift Pokémon battle

steps.title: Kristiline and the Frozen Arc
steps.description: Investigate the frozen settlement and the force controlling the region through Ice-focused Field Effects, environmental hazards, and church-like puzzle areas before a major confrontation.
steps.locations: Kristiline Town, frozen routes, church interiors
steps.battles: Angie encounters and an Ice-focused gym battle

steps.title: Grand Dream City and Darchlight
steps.description: Enter Grand Dream City, register for its competitive events, and investigate Darchlight Woods and the organizations operating beneath the city. Faction choices change later dialogue, available allies, and Karma.
steps.locations: Grand Dream City districts, Darchlight Woods, Bladestar facilities

steps.title: Paragon and Renegade Routes
steps.description: Finish late-game preparation, then choose between the Paragon path that preserves allies and the darker Renegade path shaped by accumulated choices. Keep a separate save for each route and never overwrite the pre-branch file.
steps.locations: Chapter 16 story zones and late-game Aevium
steps.battles: Route-exclusive encounters and final story bosses

rejuvenationPokemonLocations.title: Pokémon Rejuvenation Pokémon Locations
rejuvenationPokemonLocations.intro: Pokémon availability changes as new routes, cities, islands, and story permissions open. The location guide distinguishes ordinary wild encounters from gifts, trades, eggs, static events, and regional Aevian Forms, and clearly marks encounters added or moved in V14.

cards.name: Gen 1-9 Starter Pokémon
cards.description: Choose one starter during the opening laboratory sequence at Gearen Laboratory. Additional starters may require later events, trades, or New Game+ features.

cards.name: Wild Encounter Pools
cards.description: Explore grass, park areas, alleys, and time-dependent encounter zones. Species vary by area, time, weather, and story state across every chapter.

cards.name: Gifts and Trades
cards.description: Complete local requests, speak to named NPCs, and bring requested trade Pokémon. Some rewards depend on finishing sidequests before leaving the village or city arc.

cards.name: Aevian Forms
cards.description: Regional Aevian Forms have unique typings, abilities, moves, and evolution requirements, and should be tracked separately from their standard forms.

cards.name: Mystery and Sidequest Eggs
cards.description: Complete the relevant request and keep an open party slot. The resulting species may depend on a predetermined save variable, quest branch, or specific event.

cards.name: V14 New and Relocated Pokémon
cards.description: Includes Gen 9 additions, newly obtainable species, and encounters moved from older locations. Always confirm against the current V14 encounter tables.

filterTips: Filter by region, chapter, and encounter method to narrow your search.
Mark Aevian Forms separately so their unique evolution requirements stay visible.
Time-of-day and weather can change which species appear in the same area.
Cross-check older V13.5 guides because V14 redistributes many encounters.

rejuvenationPasswords.title: Pokémon Rejuvenation Passwords and Codes
rejuvenationPasswords.intro: V14 passwords are entered through the password option offered during the opening setup or through supported in-game password management. Some settings change the entire ruleset, so create a separate save before activating challenge passwords.

cards.effect: Allows supported traversal actions without permanently teaching every traditional HM-style move to the active team.
Provides earlier access to the Exp. All system so the party can share experience without waiting for its normal acquisition point.
Provides earlier access to Mega Evolution and Z-Crystal progression than a standard playthrough would normally allow.
Stops Pokémon from gaining additional levels after reaching the current badge-based level cap.
Reduces the strength and optimization of opposing teams for a more forgiving, story-focused experience.
Restricts or disables Bag items during trainer battles, forcing reliance on held items, switching, abilities, and moves.
Allows greater control over weather conditions used by overworld encounters, events, and certain battle strategies.
Makes obtainable Pokémon generate with maximized individual values, reducing breeding and stat-reset grinding.

usageNotes: Enter passwords during new-game setup or through the supported password menu.
Create a separate unmodified save before comparing challenge runs.
Some passwords cannot be safely removed after story progress begins.
Treat password-assisted saves distinctly when sharing challenge records.

rejuvenationBeginnerGuide.title: Pokémon Rejuvenation Beginner Guide
rejuvenationBeginnerGuide.intro: Pokémon Rejuvenation is a long, demanding fan game built around Field Effects, level caps, and challenging boss design. Players who understand the core systems early avoid frustration and keep their team viable across the full V14 story.

steps.title: Choose a difficulty that fits your goal
steps.description: V14 offers difficulty options that scale opposing team optimization, held items, and Field Effect usage. Pick an easier setting for a story-focused run and use the Password System to further tune the experience.
steps.pitfall: Starting on the hardest base difficulty without understanding Field Effects leads to repeated early losses.

steps.title: Respect the badge-based level cap
steps.description: Pokémon stop gaining experience once they reach the cap set by your current number of badges, unless a password changes this behavior. Plan team rotations around the cap so experience spreads across multiple members.
steps.pitfall: Pouring all experience into one Pokémon leaves the rest of the team under-leveled for consecutive boss battles.

steps.title: Learn how Field Effects change battles
steps.description: Field Effects are terrain and weather-like conditions that modify move power, type effectiveness, abilities, and secondary effects. Many gyms and bosses are designed around a specific field, so reading the tooltip and planning a counter is essential.
steps.pitfall: Ignoring the active field turns favorable type matchups into losses because move multipliers shift dramatically.

steps.title: Build a balanced early team
steps.description: Aim for broad type coverage rather than stacking same-type attackers. Include reliable STAB for common early types, one tank or pivot, and one Pokémon that can manipulate or remove Field Effects.
steps.pitfall: A mono-type early team collapses against the first gym whose field punishes that type.

steps.title: Manage money and healing items
steps.description: Currency is limited early, so spend it on healing items, status recovery, and Poké Balls. Buy a small reserve of Revives and full-status heals before each gym because several early bosses use status conditions and damage-over-time fields.
steps.pitfall: Arriving at a consecutive-boss sequence with no healing budget forces a retreat and lost progress.

steps.title: Use the Password System intentionally
steps.description: When creating a save, the Password System lets you adjust convenience, rewards, and battle rules. Activate only the passwords that match how you want to play, and keep a separate unmodified save for challenge comparisons.
steps.pitfall: Stacking many convenience passwords makes later self-imposed challenges impossible to verify.

steps.title: Track missable content as you go
steps.description: Rejuvenation contains sidequests, gifts, trades, eggs, and Crests that can become inconvenient or impossible to collect after a region is left behind. Talk to every NPC, check every building, and finish local requests before advancing.
steps.pitfall: Leaving Terajuma, Kristiline, or Grand Dream City with unfinished quests permanently removes some rewards.

steps.title: Prepare for consecutive boss battles
steps.description: Several story arcs chain multiple battles with no chance to reorder the team. Keep multiple saves, carry a balanced six-member team, and stock healing items before dungeon climaxes such as Valor Mountain and the late-game Team Xen facilities.
steps.pitfall: Saving only in one slot before a branching or chained battle can lock you into a difficult state.

rejuvenationTierList.title: Pokémon Rejuvenation Starter and Team Tier List
rejuvenationTierList.intro: Exact rankings depend on chosen difficulty, route, and playstyle, so this tier list is an evaluation framework rather than a fixed leaderboard. Use the criteria to judge any team member against the V14 content you are currently facing.

members: Starter that matches the early-game Field Effects you face
Fast attacker with strong STAB and broad coverage
tiers.reason: Available early, hits hard under common early fields, and remains useful through mid-game bosses without heavy investment.

members: Mid-game gift or trade with strong base stats
Tank or pivot that controls tempo against gym fields
Late-blooming evolved form reachable within the level cap
tiers.reason: Reliable in most major battles with some setup, though each has a type or availability limitation that keeps it below S.

members: Niche type specialist useful only in specific gyms
Strong but late-arriving member with limited early contribution
Members that need rare items or Crests to reach full potential
tiers.reason: Strong in the right matchup but requires team support, specific timing, or rare resources to outperform an A-tier pick.

members: Outclassed members with weak availability timing
Members whose best moves arrive past the current level cap
Challenge-pick members suited to self-imposed restrictions
tiers.reason: Hard to justify on a standard run because better options are available earlier and perform more consistently.

evaluationCriteria: Availability timing relative to the level cap
Synergy with active Field Effects in gyms
Boss performance against Team Xen, Rift, and gym fields
Long-term value including Mega, Z-Crystal, and Crest access
rejuvenationTierList.starterNote: All three starters are viable through V14. Pick by which early Field Effects and gym types you want to handle most smoothly.

rejuvenationBosses.title: Pokémon Rejuvenation Gym Leaders and Bosses
rejuvenationBosses.intro: Boss design in Pokémon Rejuvenation centers on Field Effects, optimized held items, and level-cap-tuned teams. Each card identifies the field or context, the team archetype, the type weaknesses to exploit, and a strategy direction.

cards.name: Early Aevium Gym Leaders
cards.field: Region-specific Field Effects introduced early to teach the mechanic
cards.weakness: Varies by leader type; bring coverage that exploits the active field
cards.strategy: Remove or counter the field first, then attack the leader's central synergy piece. Keep healing items for chained encounters.

cards.name: Goldenleaf Mansion Story Bosses
cards.weakness: Dark, Ghost, and Normal-immune tactics; watch for status and field damage
cards.strategy: Bring Dark or Ghost coverage and status recovery, and exploit the field only when it favors your types.

cards.name: Kristiline and Angie Ice Arc
cards.field: Ice and cold-themed Field Effect with hazard and freeze pressure
cards.weakness: Fire, Fighting, Rock, and Steel coverage
cards.strategy: Carry Ice-resisting attackers and Freeze heal items, and remove the cold field if your team is field-punished.

cards.name: Rift Pokémon Encounters
cards.field: Rift-themed field that boosts the corrupted Pokémon
cards.weakness: Specific to each Rift Pokémon's altered typing
cards.strategy: Treat each Rift battle as a boss: set up defensively, exploit the revealed weakness, and keep a revive reserve.

cards.name: Team Xen Administrators and Executives
cards.weakness: Fairy, Fighting, Ground, and Psychic coverage handles most Xen archetypes
cards.strategy: Identify recurring Xen members' preferred types, prepare coverage in advance, and expect optimized held items on executives.

cards.name: Late-game Gym Leaders and Climax
cards.field: Complex late-game fields with strong synergies
cards.weakness: Requires broad coverage and field control; no single-type sweep
cards.strategy: Use a fully rotated six-member team, carry Crests and Mega or Z-Crystal options, and keep separate saves before route branches.

rejuvenationItems.title: Pokémon Rejuvenation Items, TMs and Crests
rejuvenationItems.intro: Item availability in Pokémon Rejuvenation is gated by story progress, sidequests, and exploration. The guide groups items by category and notes whether each is tied to a missable quest or region.

cards.name: Technical Machines (TMs)
cards.location: Routes, cities, gyms, and sidequest rewards
cards.note: Most TMs are reusable; collect them as regions open to expand team coverage and Field Effect answers.

cards.name: Move Tutors
cards.location: Tutor NPCs in major cities and late-game hubs
cards.note: Teach exclusive moves in exchange for shards or currency. Plan which members need tutor moves before spending resources.

cards.name: Mega Stones
cards.location: Story events, sidequests, and hidden locations
cards.note: Required for Mega Evolution. Several are tied to sidequests that can be missed after leaving a region.

cards.name: Z-Crystals
cards.note: Enable Z-Moves for the matching type. Confirm availability per type before building a strategy around a specific Z-Move.

cards.name: Crests
cards.location: Hidden across Aevium and rewarded by sidequests
cards.note: Crests provide unique buffs that can make a lower-tier Pokémon viable. Treat Crest-locked members as a separate planning layer.

cards.name: Evolution and Key Items
cards.location: Shops, sidequest rewards, and main story objectives
cards.note: Includes stones, trade items, and time-of-day held items. Stock them so team members can evolve at the level cap.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Pokémon Rejuvenation V14

questions.question: What is Pokémon Rejuvenation V14?
questions.answer: Pokémon Rejuvenation V14 is a free standalone story-driven Pokémon fan game set in the Aevium region, featuring 16 chapters, Gen 1-9 Pokémon, Field Effects, Crests, and Paragon or Renegade route choices. It does not require a Pokémon ROM or emulator.

questions.question: Where can I download Pokémon Rejuvenation V14?
questions.answer: You can download Pokémon Rejuvenation V14 from the official rebornevo.com download page. Builds are available for Windows, macOS, Linux, Kirin, and JoiPlay. Always back up your V13.5 save before upgrading to V14.

questions.question: What are Field Effects in Pokémon Rejuvenation?
questions.answer: Field Effects in Pokémon Rejuvenation are terrain and weather-like conditions that modify move power, type effectiveness, abilities, and secondary effects. Many gyms and bosses are designed around a specific field, so reading and countering the active field is central to winning battles.

questions.question: What is the difference between the Paragon and Renegade routes in Pokémon Rejuvenation?
questions.answer: In Pokémon Rejuvenation, the Paragon route continues the story while preserving allies, while the Renegade route is a darker path shaped by accumulated negative choices and low Karma. Players are advised to complete the Paragon Chapter 16 first before starting the Renegade Chapter 16.

questions.question: What are Crests in Pokémon Rejuvenation?
questions.answer: Crests in Pokémon Rejuvenation are unique items hidden across Aevium that buff specific Pokémon, often making lower-tier members viable. Many Crests are tied to sidequests that can be missed after leaving a region, so collect them before advancing the story.

questions.question: How do passwords work in Pokémon Rejuvenation V14?
questions.answer: Passwords in Pokémon Rejuvenation V14 are entered during new-game setup or through the supported in-game password menu. They adjust difficulty, battle rules, rewards, and quality-of-life settings. Some passwords cannot be safely removed after story progress begins, so keep a separate unmodified save for challenge runs.
